_note: Updated daily at noon by the design agent. Plain language — no jargon.

priorities.title: Room editor UX — complete overhaul for clarity, accessibility, and usability
priorities.note: Environment phase subwizard shipped on 2026-04-05: one-step-at-a-time Environment flow, room-scoped details moved into dedicated steps, review evidence collapsed behind Preview and build, and the overlap-causing full-bleed dock width removed. Broader room editor accessibility and interaction work remains in progress.
priorities.proposed_solution: Continue the room editor overhaul in slices: keep reducing mode confusion, tighten keyboard and focus behavior, and apply the same step-by-step disclosure model to other dense room-editor surfaces where side panels still compete for space.

priorities.title: Room editor overall UX improvements
priorities.note: Address comprehensive usability, visual clarity, and user flow for the room editor to improve overall user experience.
priorities.proposed_solution: Conduct user testing, gather feedback, and implement iterative design improvements focusing on intuitiveness and accessibility.

priorities.title: Visual design enhancements for room editor
priorities.note: Improve visual clarity and consistency in the room editor interface to aid user comprehension and reduce errors.
priorities.proposed_solution: Refine color schemes, iconography, and layout to create a more intuitive and visually accessible environment.

priorities.title: Design review — Ashen Hollow art bible v0.2
priorities.note: Approve with conditions: enforce §6.1 (toolchain vs in-game UI); HUD must pair color with non-color cues; optional STYLE_GUIDE cross-link. Memo saved.
priorities.proposed_solution: Engineering treats AH-* as game-only; chrome stays STYLE_GUIDE. See docs/reports/design-review-ashen-hollow-art-bible-v0.2.md.

priorities.title: Art bible PDF / markdown email — STYLE_GUIDE dark export
priorities.note: Formal memo docs/reports/design-review-art-bible-pdf-export-2026-04-09.md (Outlook / ~18 MB attachment). Added --pdf-only + stderr WARNING when PDF ≥10 MB.
priorities.proposed_solution: Founder uses --pdf-only for local PDF; pick lite PDF or hosted link for email per memo §5

founder_decisions.title: Art bible PDF — email delivery vs local vs hosted
founder_decisions.note: Large PDFs (~18 MB) often fail in Outlook; read docs/reports/design-review-art-bible-pdf-export-2026-04-09.md and pick §5 option A/B/C.

actions.name: Design Review
actions.description: Token compliance, accessibility, and interaction compliance assessment on a PR — approve / approve with conditions / reject.
actions.trigger: Any PR touching HTML, CSS, or frontend JS

actions.name: Token Audit
actions.description: Full audit of all CSS files for off-token values: colors, spacing, radius, fonts — with file and line references.
actions.trigger: Quarterly or after any major CSS refactor

actions.name: Style Guide Proposal
actions.description: Propose a new token or component definition with rationale, migration notes, and before/after comparison.
actions.trigger: A new UI pattern is needed that isn't in the current spec

actions.name: IA Review
actions.description: Information architecture assessment of a new feature against cognitive load and mode-error principles.
actions.trigger: New tool feature or significant workflow change

opportunities.title: Full token and design-system consistency pass across the tools
opportunities.note: Replaces the separate inconsistency sweep — same outcome as a scheduled token audit when we run it.
opportunities.proposed_solution: Run the token audit across CSS, file tickets for fixes, and align with STYLE_GUIDE.md.

opportunities.title: Visual regression coverage — QA-owned automation with design input on tolerance
opportunities.note: Single priority spanning screenshot or visual checks; QA leads tooling once the editor is stable; design defines what counts as a regression.
opportunities.proposed_solution: After the room editor stabilizes, pair with QA on a minimal visual regression or screenshot gate.

opportunities.title: Review Business Brand Guide v0.2 — Design cells in §1.3 matrix
opportunities.note: Complete 2026-04-02. Approved: tokens, typography, motion, entity colors, game vs UI boundary. Hex in pamphlet OK for print; tools stay CSS vars. Follow-up: research-status still tracks product-shell marketing CSS token debt—implementation, not guide text. Memo: docs/reports/business-brand-guide-v0.2-stakeholder-reviews-2026-04-02.md.
opportunities.proposed_solution: Complete §1.3 Design checkboxes; flag conflicts with STYLE_GUIDE.md so Marketing can issue v0.3.
